title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, compared to competitors requiring four or five GPUs. Smaller variants (2B, 4B) enable deployment on phones, medical systems, and devices like the M4 Mac. The model now uses Apache 2.0 licensing instead of a custom license.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma's open-model strategy through performance and deployment claims. Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on LM Arena while running on a single GPU and measuring 2-20x smaller than competitors at comparable quality, illustrating token efficiency as a path to ownership. Smaller 2B and 4B variants target mobile deployment on Pixel phones, with the model also running on M4 Macs and hospital infrastructure. The shift from custom licensing to Apache 2.0 is framed as enabling sovereign adoption without procurement delays.
